stem: A kite is flying at the end of a 60-foot string that makes a 53-degree angle of elevation with the ground. Given that sin 53° ≈ 4/5, cos 53° ≈ 3/5, and tan 53° ≈ 4/3, what is the height of the kite above the ground, in feet?
explanation: In this right triangle, the string is the hypotenuse (60 feet) and the kite's height is the side opposite the 53° angle. Using sin 53° = opposite/hypotenuse: height = 60 × sin 53° = 60 × (4/5) = 240/5 = 48 feet.

distractorRationale.B: Used cos instead of sin — found the horizontal distance (adjacent) instead of the height (opposite): 60 × (3/5) = 36
distractorRationale.C: Set up the ratio inverted — divided by sin instead of multiplying, computing hypotenuse/sin 53° instead of hypotenuse × sin 53°: 60 ÷ (4/5) = 60 × (5/4) = 75
distractorRationale.D: Used wrong special triangle ratio — applied the 30-60-90 rule (short leg = hypotenuse/2) instead of using the given trig value: 60/2 = 30
